message:
harden(scripts): add ANSI stripping to rch log parsing, scope drift detection to coverage matrix, and update bead snapshot

RGC CI quality gates (run_rgc_ci_quality_gates.sh):
- Add rch_strip_ansi() helper to strip ANSI escape sequences from rch
  logs before parsing remote exit codes and fallback markers, preventing
  false negatives when rch daemon output contains colored terminal output
- Apply ANSI stripping to rch_remote_exit_code() and rch_reject_local_
  fallback() and the recovery path in run_step_rch()
- Expand local fallback detection patterns to catch additional rch error
  messages: "Failed to query daemon:.*running locally", "Dependency
  preflight blocked remote execution", "RCH-E326"

Verification coverage matrix gate (run_rgc_verification_coverage_matrix_gate.sh):
- Add scope.open_bead_ids snapshot validation: load bead IDs from the
  matrix JSON, verify sorted order and uniqueness
- Add snapshot drift detection: compare matrix snapshot against live
  `br list --json --limit 0` output using comm to find stale (only-in-
  snapshot) and missing (only-in-live) beads, reporting drift as a
  validation error
- Use `br list --json --limit 0` instead of bare `br list --json` to
  ensure all beads are returned without default pagination limits
- Filter live beads with `.status != "closed"` instead of explicit
  `.status == "open" or .status == "in_progress"` to correctly include
  all non-terminal statuses

Security conformance runner (run_security_conformance_runner.sh):
- Change check mode from `cargo check` to `cargo test --no-run` so that
  rch applies the longer cargo-test timeout envelope instead of the
  shorter cargo-check cap, preventing timeout-induced false failures

Coverage matrix tests:
- Make load_live_open_rgc_beads() return Option<Vec<String>> and handle
  NotFound gracefully (br not installed) instead of panicking
- Add rgc_051_critical_behavior_beads_are_within_open_scope_snapshot test
  verifying all critical behavior bead IDs are present in the scope
  snapshot
- Update snapshot_source assertion to match new `--limit 0` flag

Matrix JSON + docs update:
- Refresh scope.open_bead_ids snapshot to current live state (remove 3
  closed beads: bd-1lsy.11.12.1, bd-1lsy.11.12.2, bd-1lsy.11.15)
- Update generated_at_utc and snapshot_generated_at_utc timestamps
- Update critical_behavior_bead_ids to match refreshed scope
- Add scope drift contract documentation to RGC_VERIFICATION_COVERAGE_
  MATRIX_V1.md

Also updates .beads/issues.jsonl with latest tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -14,6 +14,8 @@ The contract is fail-closed:
 
 - every open RGC bead must match at least one row,
 - critical-behavior beads must match `unit` + `integration` + `e2e`,
+- `scope.open_bead_ids` must match live non-closed `bd-1lsy*` beads from
+  `br list --json --limit 0`,
 - every row must declare deterministic seed policy, required log fields, and
   artifact triad paths,
 - operator replay commands are first-class contract fields.

@@ -53,11 +53,16 @@ run_rch() {
     "$@"
 }
 
+rch_strip_ansi() {
+  local input="$1"
+  sed -E 's/\x1B\[[0-9;]*[[:alpha:]]//g' "$input"
+}
+
 rch_remote_exit_code() {
   local log_path="$1"
   local remote_exit_line remote_exit_code
 
-  remote_exit_line="$(rg -o 'Remote command finished: exit=[0-9]+' "$log_path" | tail -n 1 || true)"
+  remote_exit_line="$(rch_strip_ansi "$log_path" | rg -o 'Remote command finished: exit=[0-9]+' | tail -n 1 || true)"
   if [[ -z "$remote_exit_line" ]]; then
     return 1
   fi
@@ -72,7 +77,7 @@ rch_remote_exit_code() {
 
 rch_reject_local_fallback() {
   local log_path="$1"
-  if grep -Eiq 'Remote toolchain failure, falling back to local|falling back to local|fallback to local|local fallback|running locally|\[RCH\] local \(|Remote execution failed: .*running locally' "$log_path"; then
+  if rch_strip_ansi "$log_path" | grep -Eiq 'Remote execution failed: .*running locally|Remote toolchain failure, falling back to local|falling back to local|fallback to local|local fallback|running locally|\[RCH\] local \(|Failed to query daemon:.*running locally|Dependency preflight blocked remote execution|RCH-E326'; then
     echo "rch reported local fallback; refusing local execution for heavy command" >&2
     return 1
   fi
@@ -135,7 +140,7 @@ run_step_rch() {
   log_path="$(mktemp)"
 
   if ! run_rch "$@" > >(tee "$log_path") 2>&1; then
-    if rg -q "Remote command finished: exit=0" "$log_path"; then
+    if rch_strip_ansi "$log_path" | rg -q "Remote command finished: exit=0"; then
       echo "==> recovered: remote execution succeeded; artifact retrieval timed out" | tee -a "$log_path"
     else
       rm -f "$log_path"

@@ -33,6 +33,7 @@ declare -a validation_errors=()
 declare -a uncovered_beads=()
 declare -a critical_gaps=()
 declare -a active_beads=()
+declare -a snapshot_scope_beads=()
 declare -a critical_beads=()
 declare -a required_log_fields=()
 declare -a required_artifact_triad=()
@@ -131,7 +132,7 @@ validate_harness_entrypoint() {
 }
 
 load_matrix_contract() {
-  local schema_ok
+  local schema_ok bead previous_bead
 
   if [[ ! -f "$matrix_json" ]]; then
     record_error "missing matrix JSON: ${matrix_json}"
@@ -162,6 +163,23 @@ load_matrix_contract() {
     record_error "matrix missing required_artifact_triad"
   fi
 
+  mapfile -t snapshot_scope_beads < <(jq -r '(.scope.open_bead_ids // [])[]' "$matrix_json")
+  if (( ${#snapshot_scope_beads[@]} == 0 )); then
+    record_error "matrix missing scope.open_bead_ids snapshot"
+  fi
+  previous_bead=""
+  for bead in "${snapshot_scope_beads[@]}"; do
+    if [[ -n "$previous_bead" && "$bead" < "$previous_bead" ]]; then
+      record_error "scope.open_bead_ids must be sorted lexicographically"
+      break
+    fi
+    if [[ -n "$previous_bead" && "$bead" == "$previous_bead" ]]; then
+      record_error "scope.open_bead_ids must not contain duplicates (${bead})"
+      break
+    fi
+    previous_bead="$bead"
+  done
+
   mapfile -t critical_beads < <(jq -r '(.critical_behavior_bead_ids // [])[]' "$matrix_json")
 
   return 0
@@ -216,11 +234,13 @@ validate_rows() {
 
 compute_coverage() {
   local bead row kind bead_kinds kind_found
+  local -a only_in_snapshot=()
+  local -a only_in_live=()
 
   mapfile -t active_beads < <(
-    br list --json | jq -r '
+    br list --json --limit 0 | jq -r '
       .[]
-      | select((.id | startswith("bd-1lsy")) and (.status == "open" or .status == "in_progress"))
+      | select((.id | startswith("bd-1lsy")) and (.status != "closed"))
       | .id
     ' | sort -u
   )
@@ -231,6 +251,23 @@ compute_coverage() {
     return
   fi
 
+  if (( ${#snapshot_scope_beads[@]} > 0 )); then
+    mapfile -t only_in_snapshot < <(
+      comm -23 \
+        <(printf '%s\n' "${snapshot_scope_beads[@]}" | sort -u) \
+        <(printf '%s\n' "${active_beads[@]}" | sort -u)
+    )
+    mapfile -t only_in_live < <(
+      comm -13 \
+        <(printf '%s\n' "${snapshot_scope_beads[@]}" | sort -u) \
+        <(printf '%s\n' "${active_beads[@]}" | sort -u)
+    )
+
+    if (( ${#only_in_snapshot[@]} > 0 || ${#only_in_live[@]} > 0 )); then
+      record_error "scope.open_bead_ids snapshot drift: stale=[${only_in_snapshot[*]}] missing=[${only_in_live[*]}]"
+    fi
+  fi
+
   for bead in "${active_beads[@]}"; do
     kind_found=false
     for row in "${coverage_rows[@]}"; do

@@ -317,8 +317,10 @@ run_mode() {
 
   case "$selected_mode" in
     check)
-      run_step "cargo check -p frankenengine-engine --lib --bin franken_security_conformance_runner" \
-        cargo check -p frankenengine-engine --lib --bin franken_security_conformance_runner
+      # Use test compilation without execution so rch applies the longer
+      # cargo-test timeout envelope instead of the shorter cargo-check cap.
+      run_step "cargo test -p frankenengine-engine --bin franken_security_conformance_runner --test security_conformance_integration --no-run" \
+        cargo test -p frankenengine-engine --bin franken_security_conformance_runner --test security_conformance_integration --no-run
       ;;
     test)
       run_step "cargo test -p frankenengine-engine --bin franken_security_conformance_runner --test security_conformance_integration" \
